description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar energy installation featuring a single pole supporting a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels. The solar panel array consists of six individual panels arranged in a 2x3 grid, mounted on a metallic frame. The pole is anchored into a concrete or metal base set on a patch of uneven, grassy terrain, simulating a typical urban or park environment. The solar panels are tilted at an angle to optimize sunlight capture, and the mounting structure appears robust and weather-resistant. No visible electrical cabling or inverter housing is present, suggesting these components may be concealed or not modeled. The overall design is functional and utilitarian, suitable for providing renewable energy in public spaces or urban landscapes.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array on a single pole, set in grassy terrain, with six panels arranged in a 2x3 grid and a sturdy base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full rectangular face of the solar panel array and the central supporting pole anchored in the grassy base.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panels and the depth of the mounting structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panel array and the supporting pole, with the base visible in the grassy terrain.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, again showing the tilt and structure of the panels, with more of the terrain's uneven surface visible.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing forward (relative to the pole), orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted, single vertical steel or aluminum pole with a concrete or metal base
attribute_array_configuration: 2x3 grid of solar panels mounted in a single rectangular array
